key_insight: Nanometer-thick amorphous Fe-Sn films achieve unusually high anomalous-Nernst heat-flux sensitivity by combining a strong Nernst response with low thermal conductivity.
engineering_relevance: These films offer a flexible, thin-film sensing platform for heat-flux sensors with performance that surpasses many amorphous magnetic and crystalline topological materials.
method: Systematically varied Fe-Sn composition and film thickness.
Characterized structure with X-ray diffraction and Mössbauer spectroscopy.
Evaluated anomalous-Nernst heat-flux sensitivity and substrate flexibility.
evidence_signal: The optimized amorphous Fe-Sn nanofilms reached a heat-flux sensitivity of 0.37 μm/A and this performance was reproduced on polymer substrates.
watch_item: Device performance depends on maintaining the optimized composition/thickness and local short-range order, which may be sensitive to fabrication conditions.
paper_title: Amorphous Fe-Sn nanofilms for anomalous-Nernst heat-flux sensing
